input.background: Exercise 18.3 found that 43% of CMV+ patients experienced arterial restenosis within 6 months of atherectomy. In contrast, 8% of CMV-patients experienced a similar outcome. Table 18.8 contains observed counts.
input.data:
Table 18.8 Data for Exercise 18.3 and 18.9. Restenosis within 6 months of angioplasty by CMV serological status.

\begin{tabular}{lc|c|}
\hline & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ Restenosed } & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ Did not restenose } & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ Total }\
\hline
\hline CMV + & 21 & 28 & 49 \
\hline CMV- & 2 & 24 & 26 \
\hline Total & 23 & 49 & 75 \
\hline
\end{tabular}
input.question: Use a chi-square statistic to test the association for statistical significance.

output.answer:
$X_{\text {stat }}^2=9.879, d f=1, P=0.0017$

With continuity-correction, $X^2$ stat, $c=8.294, d f=1, P=0.0040$

CMV_status.description: The serological status of patients, indicating whether they are CMV positive or negative.

Restenosis_status.description: The outcome status indicating whether the patient experienced restenosis within 6 months.

CMV+_Restenosed.description: The count of CMV+ patients who experienced restenosis.

CMV+_Did_not_restenose.description: The count of CMV+ patients who did not experience restenosis.

CMV-_Restenosed.description: The count of CMV- patients who experienced restenosis.

CMV-_Did_not_restenose.description: The count of CMV- patients who did not experience restenosis.

Total_Restenosed.description: The total count of patients who experienced restenosis.

Total_Did_not_restenose.description: The total count of patients who did not experience restenosis.

Total_patients.description: The total number of patients in the study.
